Please find the new patch set.

Giving conditional +1 based on the below.
1. Discussion regarding changing from IMap to Map interface ( at the very beginning when we introduced hazelcast , we were doing it like this and were having issues in replication and TTL expiration)
2. trustLevelPerDmiPlugin should not store the trustLevel. It should keep track of the health of the DMI plugin. So the storage would be the status of dmi-plugin i.e UP or DOWN.

Above to be handled as part of separate patches once the discussion is done.
Agree with you Priyank on conditions. After those being discussed can be handled in a new commit.
Blocking until we have  had a chat with Priyank about using Map v. IMap

For the naming of this class, agree with you better to have suffix Filter at the end.

The current requirement #2: The cm handles should only be returned if their trust level is equal to trust level condition. Thus, I suggest not to implement a second method yet.

For the last question: As far as I know one of the area where the comparable interface can be used is the sorting collection of an object based on the object fields. In this case, the TrustLevel enum has no field to be sorted other than having just two values: COMPLETE and NONE as of now.

Hi Priyank, can we discuss this. we changed it to Map delibertaly so it could be tested more easily. It jsut using higher level interface if no IMap specific methods er needed. WE have doen the same for the Hazelcat Queue is well before!
Not suer what teh old issue was bug I think we might have been wrong thinking it was cause by using Map instead oif IMap... 
Halil has also doen end-2-end testing while debugging on his laptop and it all worked OPK...

Hi Toine , I know technically it totally makes sense to replace IMap with Map ( map being the parent interface of IMap )
But when we first introduced hazelcast in CPS and we did used Map instead of IMap I saw when running multiple instances , the data was not replicated and the TTL feature was not working.

It could be coz of something different back then as we were doing it for the first time.
But this is just my thinking that I had before.

I would test it locally on my own and Open for discussion obviously.

I tested on my local machine with multiple instances and came to a conclusion as below.

1. if the TTL feature is not needed then use the Map interface ( as it would just provide us with standard collection Map api's )
2. if the TTL feature is needed we use the more specialized interface i.e IMap.
As IMap provides us with APIs to put the keys with its TTL as well.

Replication works for both the cases.

As I understand , we dont need to expire the keys in this use case. So I am ok to let this one merge.

I agree that we should not need to use TTL feature since cm handle ids will  be updated when their state changed other than deleting them from the map. Also this commit is a blocker for the upcoming dev tasks. Thus If now the team agreed on based the tests made by Priyank and myself, we can proceed to merge this commit.
OK just to clarify: IMAP inerface is only needed if you want DIFFERENT TTL for different objects in the map....
